The **Men's FIH Hockey Junior World Cup**, formerly known as the **Hockey Junior World Cup**, is an international [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") competition organized by the [International Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation "International Hockey Federation"). The tournament was started in 1979. Since 1985 it was held every four years. From 2021 onwards the tournament has been held every two years. Competitors must be under the age of 21 as of December 31 in the year before the tournament is held.

There is also a corresponding event for the [women's junior teams](/wiki/Women%27s_FIH_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup "Women's FIH Hockey Junior World Cup"). This competition started in 1989 and uses the same format as the men's event.

Five countries have dominated the event's history. [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national under-21 field hockey team") is the most successful team, having won the tournament eight times followed by [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "Argentina men's national under-21 field hockey team") and [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "India men's national under-21 field hockey team"), having won the tournament two times. [Australia](/wiki/Australia_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "Australia men's national under-21 field hockey team") and [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "Pakistan men's national under-21 field hockey team") have each won the tournament once.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The 2009 Junior World Cup was held jointly between [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") and [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"), with [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national under-21 field hockey team") defeating the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_under-21_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national under-21 field hockey team") 3–1 in the final. The 2013 tournament was held in India from 2 to 17 November 2013. Germany won the final for record 6th time defeating France 5–2. France claimed their first-ever medal in the tournament winning silver after losing to Germany.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The 2016 edition was held between 8–18 December 2016 in [Lucknow](/wiki/Lucknow "Lucknow"), India, with India defeating Belgium 2–1 in the final.[[2]](#cite_note-2) India also became the first host nation to win the Junior World Cup.[[3]](#cite_note-‘Chak_De_India’:_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup_title_win_for_India_after_15_years-3) India are also the first and only nation to win any type of medal in junior world cup as a host. From 2025 onwards the tournament will expand to 24 teams.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Format")]

The Junior Hockey World Cup consists of a qualification stage and a final tournament stage. All the participating teams in the final tournament play in the qualification tournament.

### Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Qualification")]

All the teams who wish to qualify for the final tournament play in the relevant continental junior championships. Each continental federation receives at least two finals places and the FIH determines which federations will receive additional places.

### Final tournament

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Final tournament")]

The final tournament features the continental champions and other qualified teams. In the tournament in 2009, the teams played a [round robin phase](/wiki/Round_robin_tournament "Round robin tournament"), with the two top teams in each pool advancing to a medal-round and remaining teams playing for classification positions. The composition of the pools is determined using the current [world rankings](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation#World_ranking "International Hockey Federation").
